pattern: Since every wizard must begin a potion the instant the previous wizard finishes it, the earliest a potion can start at the first wizard is forced upward by comparing, against the previous potion's own cumulative timings, how far ahead each wizard would need it to already be -- so the whole schedule falls out of a single running offset carried from potion to potion.
hints: Because a potion must move to the next wizard the instant the current one finishes, once you fix when a potion starts at the very first wizard, its arrival time at every later wizard is completely determined by adding up that wizard's own processing times.

A potion cannot start at any wizard before the previous potion has already finished with that same wizard, so compare, for every wizard position, the previous potion's finishing time there against the new potion's would-be arrival time, and take whichever start satisfies every wizard at once.

Carrying forward the starting offset from one potion to the next this way, then adding the last potion's own total processing time, gives the overall completion time without ever simulating wizard-by-wizard waiting explicitly.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, four wizards and four potions with a tight schedule
statement example, uniform wizards and potions
statement example, more wizards than potions
wizard skills decreasing, the reverse of the sorted case
all values at the maximum allowed bound

stress.description: random moderate-size skill and mana arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 400))
    m = max(1, min(size, 400))
    skill = [rng.randint(1, 5000) for _ in range(n)]
    mana = [rng.randint(1, 5000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [skill, mana]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minTime(self, skill, mana):
        n = len(skill)
        m = len(mana)
        prefix_prev = []
        acc = 0
        for i in range(n):
            acc += skill[i] * mana[0]
            prefix_prev.append(acc)
        S = 0
        for j in range(1, m):
            prefix_cur = []
            acc = 0
            for i in range(n):
                acc += skill[i] * mana[j]
                prefix_cur.append(acc)
            max_val = None
            for i in range(n):
                prev_at_i = prefix_prev[i]
                cur_before = 0 if i == 0 else prefix_cur[i - 1]
                diff = prev_at_i - cur_before
                if max_val is None or diff > max_val:
                    max_val = diff
            S += max_val
            prefix_prev = prefix_cur
        return S + prefix_prev[-1]
